assertions.title: /v1/tokenizer_info returns correct metadata

steps.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON containing eos_token, bos_token, pad_token, chat_template
steps.observed: HTTP 200 with {eos_token: '<eos>', bos_token: '<bos>', pad_token: '<unk>'}. chat_template key is MISSING (not present as null, completely absent).

issues: chat_template key is absent from response. Contract requires it to be present (string or null). Source code sets info["chat_template"] = nil which drops the key in JSON serialization.

steps.observed: Both produce same [23391, 1902]. This is expected: Gemma tokenizer handles BOS at generation time, not in encode(). The tokenizer simply does not inject special tokens during encode.
HTTP 200 with text: '' - negative IDs silently produce empty text instead of 4xx error
HTTP 200 with text: '' - out-of-range IDs silently produce empty text

nonBlockingIssues: Negative and out-of-range token IDs return 200 with empty text instead of 4xx. Contract uses SHOULD (non-blocking).

assertions.title: Roundtrip: tokenize then detokenize returns original text

steps.action: Roundtrip long: 'The quick brown fox...' x5

steps.observed: LOSSY - space before ! is dropped by Gemma SentencePiece BPE. Output: 'Mixed 123 and!@# symbols'. This matches the tokenizer's inherent encode-decode behavior. Contract explicitly allows lossy behavior.

evidence.lossyBehavior: Gemma tokenizer's SentencePiece BPE merges space before punctuation like ! into the punctuation token. This is inherent tokenizer behavior, not a server bug. Contract says: 'the result MUST match the tokenizer's own decode(encode(text)) behavior exactly' - which it does.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed, 1 failed. VAL-TOK-001 fails because chat_template key is missing from /v1/tokenizer_info response. VAL-TOK-002, VAL-TOK-003, VAL-TOK-004 all pass. Note: Gemma tokenizer has known lossy space-before-punctuation behavior in roundtrip, which is inherent to SentencePiece BPE and allowed by contract.
